sourceMovie.title: Jeremiah Johnson
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Jeremiah Johnson (1972) stuck because it commits to its adventure premise without apologizing—viewers remember set pieces and momentum as much as plot. Sydney Pollack's control of tone keeps the ride coherent. The adventure and western blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: The Revenant
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Jeremiah Johnson clicked for you, The Revenant (2015) is a strong next stop: both trade in adventure and western, with momentum and clean stakes. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Jeremiah Johnson?
faq.answer: Try The Revenant, True Grit, Dances with Wolves—each shares DNA with Jeremiah Johnson while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Jeremiah Johnson?
faq.answer: Jeremiah Johnson (1972) is best described as Adventure, Western, Drama, Action. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Jeremiah Johnson worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 7.3/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this adventure lane.
